question: Compare the SG design parameters from Table 5.4-1 with the RPV design parameters from Table 5.3-1.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which is surrounded by a containment vessel (CNV). The RPV serves as a key pressure-retaining component, housing critical reactor systems and providing structural support, while being constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure long-term integrity and safety.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components in the NuScale reactor, specifying alloys and standards for each part to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments. It references relevant sections of the BPVC (Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code) and lists the specific alloys and material specifications for the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, heater sheaths, and bolting.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: This section describes the evaluation of the NuScale Power Plant's systems to optimize performance across various power levels. Key operational parameters—such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures—are established and verified using both software and manual calculations, accounting for uncertainties and ensuring efficient, safe operation. The design supports natural circulation with minimal pressure losses, and uncertainties related to steam generator tube plugging do not affect primary coolant flow determination.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: Each NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel contains two integral steam generators, which are part of the reactor coolant system and share the vessel’s pressure boundary. The steam generator system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and internal plenums, with the reactor pressure vessel itself forming the shell and pressure boundary for the steam generators.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel throughout its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to maintain pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that all predicted values remain within regulatory safety criteria.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety valves, and associated piping and valves. It details the integration of these components within the reactor vessel and outlines the boundaries between the primary and secondary sides of the steam generator system, emphasizing the RPV’s role as the pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes the main components of the NuScale reactor system, focusing on the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping, and the Pressurizer (PZR). The RPV serves as a primary barrier to fission product release and houses key reactor internals, while the RCS piping connects various system elements, and the PZR is integrated into the RPV to help control system pressure. Each component's functions and connections are briefly outlined, with references to further detailed sections.

edges.description: Section A describes the overall configuration and function of the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), which houses the steam generators (SGs), while Section B provides detailed material specifications for components of the pressurizer that are part of the RPV, thus specifying details for the general RPV description in Section A.

Section A describes the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) as containing the steam generators (SGs), while Section B discusses the establishment of steady state design parameters for the SGs and references their further description, thus connecting both sections through the design parameters of the RPV and SGs as components of the NuScale Power Module.

Section A describes the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) as containing two steam generators (SGs), while Section B specifies that the RPV houses the SGs along with other components, establishing that the SGs' design parameters are contextually linked to and constrained by the RPV's design parameters.

Section A describes the physical configuration and role of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B addresses the operational procedures developed to ensure that the RPV's pressure-temperature limits—parameters specified in design tables—are not exceeded, directly connecting the design values from Section A to the compliance measures in Section B.

Section A describes the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and its role in housing the steam generators (SGs), while Section B elaborates on the RPV's internal components, including the SGs, thus both sections provide complementary details about the physical integration and design context of the SGs and RPV, which is necessary for comparing their design parameters.

Section B describes how steady state design parameters for the SG are established for optimal performance, while Section A references the RPV design and materials, connecting them through the need to compare and align SG and RPV parameters for system compatibility and safety.

Section B describes the overall configuration of the RPV, explicitly stating that it contains both the pressurizer (detailed in Section A) and the steam generators, thereby establishing that the SG and RPV design parameters are directly connected as components within the same integrated system.

Section A describes the materials and components of the pressurizer, which is part of the RPV upper shell, while Section B discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure the RPV's pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, connecting them through the shared concern for RPV integrity and operational safety.

Section A provides detailed material specifications for components that are part of the RPV described in Section B, specifically elaborating on the pressurizer and its integration within the RPV as outlined in the general system design.

Section A references Section B by directing the reader to Section 5.4 for further details on the steam generators, establishing a structural connection between the system evaluation and the specific component design.

Section A discusses the establishment of steady state design parameters for the steam generators (SGs) to optimize plant performance, while Section B addresses the development of operating procedures to ensure the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) design parameters, specifically pressure-temperature limits, are not exceeded; both sections are connected through their focus on defining and maintaining design parameters for key plant components under operational conditions.

Section A discusses the establishment of steady state design parameters for the steam generators (SGs) to optimize plant performance, while Section B describes the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and its integration with the SGs, indicating that the SG and RPV design parameters are interrelated components within the overall reactor coolant system design.

The SG design parameters from Table 5.4-1 must be compatible with the RPV design parameters from Table 5.3-1 to ensure that operating procedures can maintain pressure-temperature limits and overall reactor vessel integrity.

Section A and Section B both describe the components contained within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), specifically noting that the RPV houses two steam generators (SGs), establishing that the SGs are integral subsystems within the RPV's design parameters.

Section B describes the components and subsystems contained within the reactor pressure vessel, including the steam generators, while Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure that the reactor vessel's pressure-temperature limits (design parameters) are not exceeded, thus connecting the design parameters of both the SG and RPV as critical to safe operation.

Section A describes the system components and operational procedures involving the steam generators (SGs), while Section B provides the design context of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) that houses the SGs, connecting them as co-located major components within the NuScale Power Module.

Section A discusses operational procedures involving the steam generators (SGs) and reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B provides material specifications for RPV components, connecting them through their respective design and operational roles in the reactor system.

Section B discusses the establishment of steady state design parameters for both the SG and RPV to optimize plant performance, which are then concretely listed in Tables 5.4-1 (SG) and 5.3-1 (RPV) referenced in the analysis question, thus connecting the evaluation criteria to the specific component parameters described in Section A.
